primary.title: Percentage Calculator - Free Online Math Tool
primary.description: Calculate percentages, percentage values, fractions, and percentage differences instantly with our free client-side tool.
primary.heading: Percentage Calculator
primary.seo_content:
This calculator supports five essential percentage computations. Here is a quick reference guide with formulas and real-life examples:

  • 1. Find Percentage Value (X% of Y)

    Formula: (X × Y) / 100
    Example: Finding a 15% tip on a $120 bill. The result is $18.00.

  • 2. Find Part Ratio (X is what % of Y?)

    Formula: (X / Y) × 100
    Example: Answering 35 questions correctly out of 50 on a test. The result is 70%.

  • 3. Percentage Change (Increase/Decrease)

    Formula: ((End - Start) / Start) × 100
    Example: Your stock value grows from $80 to $100. This is a 25% increase.

  • 4. Percentage Difference

    Formula: (|X - Y| / ((X + Y) / 2)) × 100
    Example: Comparing a $50 product with a similar $80 product. The difference is 46.15%.

  • 5. Find Base Value (X is Y% of what?)

    Formula: (X / Y) × 100
    Example: If a downpayment of $15 is 25% of the total cost, the total is $60.00.

examples.title: Finding a percentage of a value
examples.description: If you want to find 15% of a $120 dinner bill, enter 15 in the rate field and 120 in the total field. The result is $18.

examples.title: Calculating ratio parts
examples.description: If a student answers 35 questions correctly out of 50, enter 35 in the part field and 50 in the whole field. The result is 70%.

faqs.question: What is the basic formula for a percentage?
faqs.answer: The basic mathematical formula is: Percentage = (Part / Whole) × 100. For example, 20 out of 80 is (20/80) × 100 = 25%.

faqs.question: How do you turn a decimal into a percentage?
faqs.answer: To convert any decimal number to a percentage, simply multiply the decimal by 100 and add the percentage sign. For example, 0.45 becomes 45% (0.45 × 100).

percentage-increase-calculator.title: Percentage Increase Calculator - Solve Growth Percentage
percentage-increase-calculator.description: Determine the percentage increase from one value to another. Learn the formula and see step-by-step math examples.
percentage-increase-calculator.heading: Percentage Increase Calculator
percentage-increase-calculator.seo_content:
A percentage increase measures the rate of growth from an original starting value to a final value. Here is a quick reference guide:

  • Percentage Growth Formula

    Formula: ((New Value - Old Value) / Old Value) × 100
    Example: If your hourly rate goes from $30 to $36, the increase is (($36 - $30) / $30) × 100 = 20% increase.

examples.title: Salary raise calculation
examples.description: If your hourly rate goes from $30 to $36, the starting value is 30 and the ending value is 36. This is a 20% salary increase.

examples.title: Website visitor growth
examples.description: If your monthly blog readers grow from 1,200 to 1,800, the starting value is 1200 and the ending value is 1800. This represents a 50% traffic increase.

faqs.question: What is the percentage increase formula?
faqs.answer: The formula to calculate percentage increase is: % Increase = ((New Value - Old Value) / Old Value) × 100. For example, if a price grows from $50 to $65, the increase is (($65 - $50) / $50) × 100 = 30%.

faqs.question: Can percentage increase exceed 100%?
faqs.answer: Yes, if a value more than doubles, the percentage increase will exceed 100%. For instance, if an amount grows from 10 to 30, it is a 200% increase.

percentage-decrease-calculator.title: Percentage Decrease Calculator - Find Rate of Drop
percentage-decrease-calculator.description: Calculate the percentage drop or decrease from a starting number to a final number. Formula and examples included.
percentage-decrease-calculator.heading: Percentage Decrease Calculator
percentage-decrease-calculator.seo_content:
A percentage decrease measures the rate of reduction from a starting value to a final lower value. Here is a quick reference guide:

  • Percentage Drop Formula

    Formula: ((Original Value - Final Value) / Original Value) × 100
    Example: If stock value drops from $100 to $70, the decrease is (($100 - $70) / $100) × 100 = 30% decrease.

examples.title: Retail store sale
examples.description: If a winter jacket is marked down from $120 to $90, the initial price is 120 and the final price is 90. This represents a 25% price decrease.

examples.title: Weight loss tracker
examples.description: If an athlete's body weight goes from 80 kg to 72 kg, the starting weight is 80 and the final weight is 72. This indicates a 10% decrease.

faqs.question: What is the percentage decrease formula?
faqs.answer: The formula to calculate percentage decrease is: % Decrease = ((Original Value - Final Value) / Original Value) × 100. For example, if stock drops from 100 to 70, the decrease is ((100 - 70) / 100) × 100 = 30%.

faqs.question: How does percentage decrease differ from percentage increase?
faqs.answer: Percentage decrease measures the rate of reduction from a higher start point to a lower endpoint, while percentage increase measures growth from a lower start point to a higher endpoint.
